speaker: VALENTINE
text: Cease to persuade, my loving Proteus.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Home-keeping youth have ever homely wits.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Were ’t not affection chains thy tender days

speaker: VALENTINE
text: To the sweet glances of thy honored love,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: I rather would entreat thy company

speaker: VALENTINE
text: To see the wonders of the world abroad

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Than, living dully sluggardized at home,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Wear out thy youth with shapeless idleness.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: But since thou lov’st, love still and thrive therein,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Even as I would when I to love begin.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Wilt thou be gone? Sweet Valentine, adieu.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Think on thy Proteus when thou haply seest

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Some rare noteworthy object in thy travel.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Wish me partaker in thy happiness

speaker: PROTEUS
text: When thou dost meet good hap; and in thy danger,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: If ever danger do environ thee,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Commend thy grievance to my holy prayers,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: For I will be thy beadsman, Valentine.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And on a love-book pray for my success?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Upon some book I love I’ll pray for thee.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: That’s on some shallow story of deep love,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: How young Leander crossed the Hellespont.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: That’s a deep story of a deeper love,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: For he was more than over shoes in love.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: ’Tis true, for you are over boots in love,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And yet you never swam the Hellespont.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Over the boots? Nay, give me not the boots.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: No, I will not, for it boots thee not.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: What?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: To be in love, where scorn is bought with groans,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Coy looks with heart-sore sighs, one fading moment’s mirth

speaker: VALENTINE
text: With twenty watchful, weary, tedious nights;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: If haply won, perhaps a hapless gain;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: If lost, why then a grievous labor won;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: How ever, but a folly bought with wit,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Or else a wit by folly vanquishèd.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: So, by your circumstance, you call me fool.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: So, by your circumstance, I fear you’ll prove.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: ’Tis love you cavil at; I am not Love.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Love is your master, for he masters you;

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And he that is so yokèd by a fool

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Methinks should not be chronicled for wise.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Yet writers say: as in the sweetest bud

speaker: PROTEUS
text: The eating canker dwells, so eating love

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Inhabits in the finest wits of all.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And writers say: as the most forward bud

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Is eaten by the canker ere it blow,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Even so by love the young and tender wit

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Is turned to folly, blasting in the bud,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Losing his verdure, even in the prime,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And all the fair effects of future hopes.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: But wherefore waste I time to counsel thee

speaker: VALENTINE
text: That art a votary to fond desire?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Once more adieu. My father at the road

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Expects my coming, there to see me shipped.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And thither will I bring thee, Valentine.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Sweet Proteus, no. Now let us take our leave.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: To Milan let me hear from thee by letters

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Of thy success in love, and what news else

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Betideth here in absence of thy friend.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And I likewise will visit thee with mine.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: All happiness bechance to thee in Milan.

speaker: VALENTINE
text: As much to you at home. And so farewell.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: He after honor hunts, I after love.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: He leaves his friends, to dignify them more;

speaker: PROTEUS
text: I leave myself, my friends, and all, for love.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Thou, Julia, thou hast metamorphosed me,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Made me neglect my studies, lose my time,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: War with good counsel, set the world at nought;

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Made wit with musing weak, heart sick with thought.

speaker: SPEED
text: Sir Proteus, ’save you. Saw you my master?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But now he parted hence to embark for Milan.

speaker: SPEED
text: Twenty to one, then, he is shipped already,

speaker: SPEED
text: And I have played the sheep in losing him.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Indeed a sheep doth very often stray,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: An if the shepherd be awhile away.

speaker: SPEED
text: You conclude that my master is a shepherd,

speaker: SPEED
text: then, and I a sheep?

speaker: PROTEUS
text: I do.

speaker: SPEED
text: Why, then my horns are his horns, whether I

speaker: SPEED
text: wake or sleep.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: A silly answer, and fitting well a sheep.

speaker: SPEED
text: This proves me still a sheep.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: True, and thy master a shepherd.

speaker: SPEED
text: Nay, that I can deny by a circumstance.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: It shall go hard but I’ll prove it by another.

speaker: SPEED
text: The shepherd seeks the sheep, and not the

speaker: SPEED
text: sheep the shepherd; but I seek my master, and my

speaker: SPEED
text: master seeks not me. Therefore I am no sheep.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: The sheep for fodder follow the shepherd; the

speaker: PROTEUS
text: shepherd for food follows not the sheep. Thou for

speaker: PROTEUS
text: wages followest thy master; thy master for wages

speaker: PROTEUS
text: follows not thee. Therefore thou art a sheep.

speaker: SPEED
text: Such another proof will make me cry “baa.”

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But dost thou hear? Gav’st thou my letter to

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Julia?

speaker: SPEED
text: Ay, sir. I, a lost mutton, gave your letter to her, a

speaker: SPEED
text: laced mutton, and she, a laced mutton, gave me, a

speaker: SPEED
text: lost mutton, nothing for my labor.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Here’s too small a pasture for such store of

speaker: PROTEUS
text: muttons.

speaker: SPEED
text: If the ground be overcharged, you were best

speaker: SPEED
text: stick her.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Nay, in that you are astray; ’twere best pound

speaker: PROTEUS
text: you.

speaker: SPEED
text: Nay, sir, less than a pound shall serve me for

speaker: SPEED
text: carrying your letter.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: You mistake; I mean the pound, a pinfold.

speaker: SPEED
text: From a pound to a pin? Fold it over and over,

speaker: SPEED
text: ’Tis threefold too little for carrying a letter to your lover.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: But what said she?

speaker: SPEED
text: Ay.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Nod—“Ay.” Why, that’s “noddy.”

speaker: SPEED
text: You mistook, sir. I say she did nod, and you ask

speaker: SPEED
text: me if she did nod, and I say “ay.”

speaker: PROTEUS
text: And that set together is “noddy.”

speaker: SPEED
text: Now you have taken the pains to set it together,

speaker: SPEED
text: take it for your pains.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: No, no, you shall have it for bearing the letter.

speaker: SPEED
text: Well, I perceive I must be fain to bear with you.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Why, sir, how do you bear with me?

speaker: SPEED
text: Marry, sir, the letter, very orderly, having nothing

speaker: SPEED
text: but the word “noddy” for my pains.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Beshrew me, but you have a quick wit.

speaker: SPEED
text: And yet it cannot overtake your slow purse.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Come, come, open the matter in brief. What

speaker: PROTEUS
text: said she?

speaker: SPEED
text: Open your purse, that the money and the matter

speaker: SPEED
text: may be both at once delivered.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Well, sir, here is for your

speaker: PROTEUS
text: pains. What said she?

speaker: SPEED
text: Truly, sir, I think you’ll

speaker: SPEED
text: hardly win her.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Why? Couldst thou perceive so much from

speaker: PROTEUS
text: her?

speaker: SPEED
text: Sir, I could perceive nothing at all from her, no,

speaker: SPEED
text: not so much as a ducat for delivering your letter.

speaker: SPEED
text: And being so hard to me that brought your mind, I

speaker: SPEED
text: fear she’ll prove as hard to you in telling your mind.

speaker: SPEED
text: Give her no token but stones, for she’s as hard as

speaker: SPEED
text: steel.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: What said she? Nothing?

speaker: SPEED
text: No, not so much as “Take this for thy pains.”

speaker: SPEED
text: To testify your bounty, I thank you, you have

speaker: SPEED
text: testerned me. In requital whereof, henceforth

speaker: SPEED
text: carry your letters yourself. And so, sir, I’ll commend

speaker: SPEED
text: you to my master.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Go, go, begone, to save your ship from wrack,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Which cannot perish having thee aboard,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Being destined to a drier death on shore.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: I must go send some better messenger.

speaker: PROTEUS
text: I fear my Julia would not deign my lines,

speaker: PROTEUS
text: Receiving them from such a worthless post.
